questions.question: Which inequality is correct?
questions.explanation: -5 < -2 is correct. On the number line, -5 is to the LEFT of -2, so -5 is less than -2.

questions.question: Order from smallest to largest: 0.5, -1, 2, -3
questions.explanation: Reading the number line from left to right: -3, -1, 0.5, 2. Left is smaller, right is larger.

questions.question: If you multiply both sides of 4 < 6 by -1, what do you get?
questions.explanation: When multiplying by a NEGATIVE number, the inequality sign REVERSES. So 4 < 6 becomes -4 > -6. Verify: -4 is to the right of -6.

questions.question: Which number is between -2 and 3?
questions.explanation: 0 lies between -2 and 3. We can write: -2 < 0 < 3. All other options are outside this range.
